title: Maximum Score

content.problemStatement:
You are given NNN integer sequences A1,A2,…,ANA_{1}, A_{2}, \ldots, A_{N}A1​,A2​,…,AN​.

Each of these sequences contains NNN elements.

You should pick NNN elements, one from each sequence; let the element picked from sequence AiA_{i}Ai​ be denoted by EiE_{i}Ei​.

For each i  (2≤i≤N)i \; (2 \leq i \leq N)i(2≤i≤N), the condition
[
Ei>Ei−1E_{i} > E_{i-1}Ei​>Ei−1​
]
must hold.

Compute the maximum possible value of
[
E1+E2+⋯+EN.E_{1} + E_{2} + \cdots + E_{N}.E1​+E2​+⋯+EN​.
]

If it is impossible to pick such elements E1,E2,…,ENE_{1}, E_{2}, \ldots, E_{N}E1​,E2​,…,EN​, print −1-1−1 instead.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case: first line contains an integer N. Then N lines follow, each containing N space‑separated integers A[i][j] (1 ≤ i, j ≤ N).
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing the maximum possible sum E1+E2+⋯+EN satisfying E_i > E_{i-1} for all i≥2. If no such selection exists, print -1.

sampleTestCases.explanation: Pick 3 from the first row, 6 from the second row and 9 from the third row. The sum is 3+6+9=18, which is maximal.
Pick 2 from the first row and 4 from the second row. The sum is 2+4=6.
Only one element can be chosen, so the answer is 1.

solution.approach: For each row we sort its elements increasingly. Let dp[i][j] be the maximum sum achievable up to row i when we pick the j‑th smallest element of row i. For the first row dp[0][j] = sortedRow0[j]. For any later row i and element value v = sortedRowi[j], we need the best previous element that is strictly smaller than v. Because the previous row is also sorted, we can binary‑search the last index k with sortedRow(i‑1)[k] < v and take the maximum dp[i‑1][k] among all such k. To obtain this maximum in O(1) we keep a prefix‑maximum array of dp[i‑1]. Thus each row is processed in O(N log N) (binary search for each of its N elements). Overall complexity per test case is O(N^2 log N) which easily fits the limits (N ≤ 700). The algorithm uses only two rows of DP and a few auxiliary arrays, so the memory consumption is O(N).
solution.timeComplexity: O(N^2 log N) per test case (sorting each row O(N log N) and processing each element with a binary search).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N) additional memory besides the input matrix.
